name: MITRE.LIN.T1070.RULE Indicator Removal on Host
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F uid!=0 -F auid!=-1 -F path=/var/log -k siem-removal-logs
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S rename,rmdir,unlink,unlinkat,renameat -F uid!=0 -F auid!=-1 -F path=/var/log/<folder> -k siem-removal-logs

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-removal-logs
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1070.RULE Indicator Removal on Host on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-removal-logs
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1070.RULE Indicator Removal on Host

Event description: Adversaries may delete or alter generated artifacts on a host system, including logs and potentially captured files such as quarantined malware.

See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1070
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
